title: What Germany Can Learn in Energy Policy from Its Neighbors
subtitle: Institutions, price, and pragmatism: what France, Great Britain, and Italy demonstrate
excerpt: Germany is neither hopelessly behind in energy policy nor automatically the benchmark. Three lessons from comparing Britain, France, and Italy.

title: Can Religious Ideas Hinder Ethical Development? Why Social Egoism May Not Be the End of Morality
subtitle: Why social egoism may not be the end of morality, but rather its realistic beginning
excerpt: If images of God mirror human weaknesses and the self is more social than moral ideals assume — where does ethical development actually begin?

title: Authoritarian or Liberal Constitutional? Why Liberal Democracy Remains the Stronger Form of Government
subtitle: Why liberal democracy remains the stronger form of government despite all its burdens
excerpt: Speed does not determine good government — reliability, capacity to learn, and protection against arbitrariness do. Why liberal democracy remains the stronger form of government in the long run.

title: Counterfactual Scenario: How Would Globalization Have Developed for Germany and the EU if China Had Behaved More Reciprocally?
subtitle: Openness is not the same thing as reciprocity
excerpt: How would China, Germany, and the EU have developed if China's rise had been less export-driven and less price-aggressive? An evidence-based thought experiment.

title: Retirement at 70? Why Germany's Social Reality Argues Against a Simple Answer
subtitle: Life expectancy, shift work, and the unequal burden of longer working lives
excerpt: Pension policy based on averages overlooks the fact that manual workers, low earners, and shift workers die younger on average — and face much worse re-hiring odds.

title: The Next Ten Years of AI and Robotics
subtitle: Two plausible 2036 scenarios
excerpt: Software agents, humanoid robots, and geopolitical rivalry: what happens if we project the direction of the last ten years into the next ten?

title: The Impact of Globalisation on the Labour and Housing Market
subtitle: Globalisation, the labour market, and home ownership in Germany
excerpt: Globalisation did not simply make Germany poorer — but it distributed gains broadly while concentrating losses sharply on specific groups.

title: The Impact of EU Eastern Enlargement on the Labour and Housing Market
subtitle: Winners, losers, and social consequences
excerpt: EU Eastern Enlargement did not simply destroy jobs — but it noticeably increased pressure on certain segments of the German labour market.

title: The Rise of the AfD in the Context of Labour and Housing Market Developments
subtitle: Who bears the costs of openness — and who benefits
excerpt: Globalisation and EU Eastern Enlargement deepened a new social fault line — and in doing so prepared the ground for the rise of the far right.

title: The Refugee Crisis, Economic Change, and the Rise of the AfD
subtitle: Why economic change prepared the ground for the political shift to the right
excerpt: The refugee crisis did not create new discontent — it politically accelerated and made visible discontent that was already there.

title: A Fictional War Scenario: What a Major Conflict with China, Russia, and Iran Would Mean for Germany and the EU
subtitle: What a major conflict with China, Russia, and Iran would mean
excerpt: What would happen if Germany and the EU were simultaneously confronted with a major conflict involving China, Russia, and Iran? An analysis of real dependencies.

title: How Germany Could Stabilise Its Society and Become More Strategically Independent
subtitle: Housing, opportunities for upward mobility, and fewer strategic dependencies
excerpt: Germany does not need closure — it needs a pragmatic reordering of its social contract under the conditions of a more insecure world.

title: European Social Democracy Since 2000: Openness, Social Security, and the Price of Unequal Globalisation
subtitle: Openness, social security, and the price of unequal globalisation
excerpt: Social democracy didn't fail because it embraced Europe — it failed where it could no longer translate open markets into social protection.

title: Why the Culture War Could Become So Bitter
subtitle: Why economic pressure and identity conflict reinforce each other
excerpt: Economic pressure, the refugee crisis, and identity politics all contributed to Germany's bitter culture war — but none was sufficient alone.

title: Moralisation, Distinction, and Material Inequality
subtitle: On the political culture of privileged milieus in Germany
excerpt: Why privileged milieus in Germany tend towards moralisation — and how this intertwines with material inequality and social distinction.

title: Germany's Uneven Turn: East, West, and the Social Roots of the AfD
subtitle: The rise of the AfD as a cumulative process of material insecurity and representation deficit
excerpt: The AfD did not grow out of one grievance alone. Its rise was prepared by a longer social shift — and the East gave this politics its sharpest form.

title: Germany's Quiet Social Turning Point, 1997–2006
subtitle: Housing, unemployment, privatization, and Agenda 2010 in the decade around the euro cash changeover
excerpt: The years around the euro cash changeover changed Germany more quietly: a renter-majority society, the sale of public housing, high unemployment, and welfare reforms that tied security to conditionality.

title: Schizophrenia – The New Weapon in the Fight Against Terrorism
subtitle: On a quiet war that hardly anyone knows about
excerpt: How Western intelligence agencies used cutting-edge technology to neutralise potential terrorist threats — and what went wrong along the way.
